items.title: Ebay rejects $56 billion US bid by GameStop, calling it 'neither credible nor attractive'
items.summary_auto: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen has argued that by combining GameStop and eBay, he could cut costs and find synergies to create a much bigger enterprise. But analysts and investors had wondered whether the offer by GameStop, which is much smaller than the company its trying to buy, stood a chance.

items.title: What to know ahead of the FIFA World Cup
items.summary_auto: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to Canada — and it's going to be the biggest in the competition's history. It's the first to be hosted in three countries — and Canada is hoping it'll also have its first-ever World Cup win.

items.title: Alberta's chief electoral officer wants pause on sharing electors list until law amended
items.summary_auto: The province’s chief electoral officer says the province’s list of electors shouldn’t be shared with any political parties until the legislature makes changes to better protect Albertans’ personal information.

items.title: Should Canadian airports be privatized?
items.summary_auto: The federal government is mulling the privatization of Canadian airports. What are the true costs of a potential windfall?

items.title: Do a tick check every time you spend time outside, Guelph and area health unit says
items.summary_auto: Wellington-Dufferin-Guelph Public Health has updated their tick guidance as the population of arachnids grows locally and they're found in more locations, including in people's gardens.

items.title: 'It has been devastating,' U.S. spirits group says about Canadian alcohol boycotts
items.summary_auto: Between some provinces not selling American alcohol — part of the response to the trade war started by the U.S. — and consumers choosing not to buy it, the actions have been "devastating," says the CEO of the Distilled Spirits Council of the United States.

items.title: Elliot Page gave $25K for a community well in N.S. It's still not running and he's not happy
items.summary_auto: Nova Scotia-born actor Elliot Page donated $25,000 for a well for people who live near Shelburne’s old garbage dump and fear contaminated water and higher cancer rates. The project has been fraught with delays, disagreement and division.

items.title: British PM Starmer tells cabinet meeting he intends to remain leader despite calls to resign
items.summary_auto: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer defied calls to resign on Tuesday, telling ministers he would "get on with governing" despite a "destabilizing" 48 hours of growing calls to set out ​a timetable for his departure after an election drubbing.

items.title: Heated Rivalry is getting a musical parody the director calls a 'love letter' to hit Canadian series
items.summary_auto: A fast-tracked musical parody that transforms Heated Rivalry, the queer Canadian hockey romance, into an off-Broadway production complete with a "wine-mom" narrator and campy showtunes that creators hope will feed a fandom hungry for more.

items.title: As Trump and Xi meet in Beijing, the biggest 'win' may be the summit itself
items.summary_auto: As U.S. President Donald Trump travels to Beijing to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the expectations around the highly anticipated summit between the leaders of the world’s two largest economies are being tempered by the war against Iran, and as China appears to hold some key leverage.

items.title: Hantavirus doesn't have 'pandemic potential,' says health official. Why those fears remain
items.summary_auto: Public health officials have been clear that the hantavirus outbreak is not another COVID-19. But despite reassurances from experts in Canada and abroad that the vuris is not easily transmissible between humans, news of the passengers exposed on the MV Hondius cruise ship has triggered memories of the COVID-19 pandemic and fears of mass infections and lockdowns.

items.title: Boys are falling behind in school, and some experts say it starts in kindergarten
items.summary_auto: A new report submitted to the Quebec government says boys are "systemically disadvantaged" in their school system. Meanwhile, new Health Canada data shows higher rates of young men aged 15 to 24 suffer from mental health issues.

items.title: Resilient Ottawa Charge looking for Walter Cup redemption
items.summary_auto: The Ottawa Charge learned valuable lessons in last year's Walter Cup final loss to the Minnesota Frost.

items.title: Why Trump's plan to drop gas tax may leave U.S. drivers feeling empty
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is finally acknowledging the financial pain that Americans are feeling with the soaring cost of gas, but his plan to pause the federal fuel tax may not have the kind of impact either he or drivers hope for.

items.title: The cruise ship hantavirus outbreak offered a real-life simulation exercise. Did we pass?
items.summary_auto: Hantavirus took hold on a weeks-long cruise before global health officials had any idea that a rodent-borne illness was silently spreading. More people keep falling ill back on land. Scientists expect the outbreak will fizzle out, but the crisis has offered a reminder that viruses can spread faster than public health efforts.

items.title: Trans rights group says some U.S. students denied care at McGill clinic due to Trump executive order
items.summary_auto: A group that advocates for trans health care says McGill University’s wellness hub is refusing to provide hormone replacement therapy to U.S. students under the age of 19 because of fears of repercussions from the Trump administration.

items.title: 1 dead following shooting in broad daylight at Calgary grocery store parking lot
items.summary_auto: One man is dead following a shooting in the parking lot of the North Hill Co-op in the city's northeast on Monday.

items.title: 1 dead following shooting in broad daylight at Calgary Co-op parking lot: police
items.summary_auto: One man is dead following a shooting in the parking lot of the North Hill Co-op in the city's northeast on Monday.

items.title: Akwesasne man pleads guilty in smuggling case that left 9 dead in St. Lawrence River
items.summary_auto: A 35-year-old man has pleaded guilty in the United States for his role in a human smuggling conspiracy that led to the deaths of nine people, including two children under the age of three, in the St. Lawrence River.

items.title: Trial begins for owner charged in Edmonton dog attack that killed 11-year-old boy
items.summary_auto: A trial has begun at the Edmonton Court of King’s Bench for a pet owner charged in connection with a dog attack that killed an 11-year-old boy on April 1, 2024.

items.title: Fast-moving wildfire threatens 2 First Nations in southeast Sask.
items.summary_auto: Officials in southeast Saskatchewan are warning residents of a "fast-moving wildfire" that destroyed some minor structures and threatened several homes on Star Blanket Cree Nation and Little Black Bear First Nation.

items.title: Out-of-control wildfire spurs evacuation order for area near Whitecourt, Alta.
items.summary_auto: A wildfire burning out of control in north-central Alberta has prompted an evacuation order for part of Woodlands County.

items.title: Israeli parliament backs death penalty tribunal for Palestinians accused in Oct. 7 attack
items.summary_auto: Israeli lawmakers approved a bill on Monday setting up a special tribunal with the authority to try and sentence to death Palestinians convicted of taking part in the 2023 Hamas-led attack that triggered the war in Gaza.

items.title: Which rodents carry hantavirus in Canada? Your questions answered
items.summary_auto: Now that most of the passengers of a luxury cruise ship are back in their home countries after a deadly outbreak was declared on board, questions and concerns about the illness continue to circulate. CBC News put some of your most asked hantavirus questions to the experts.

items.title: Digital health records system launches without major issues, but concerns remain
items.summary_auto: Santé Québec and health authority officials described the launch of the Dossier Santé Numérique in two regional health authorities as ‘wonderful,’ though others have reported bugs.

items.title: Trans Mountain and its federal parent see case for Ottawa owning pipeline for good
items.summary_auto: There's a case to indefinitely keep the Trans Mountain pipeline in government hands, possibly alongside Indigenous partners, say the leaders of its operator and financial overseer.

items.title: Ground zero: NATO’s drone war comes down to earth in Latvia
items.summary_auto: Latvian and Canadian troops are testing battlefield ground drones on a major NATO exercise, drawing directly on Ukrainian combat lessons as autonomous warfare rapidly evolves. The war games are unfolding amid a political crisis triggered by a wayward Ukrainian drone strike — a stark warning of how electronic warfare and unmanned systems are reshaping modern conflict.

items.title: Judge orders post-prison supervision for Calgary high-risk offender who cut off girlfriend's fingers
items.summary_auto: A Calgary man who left his ex-girlfriend disabled after attacking her with a sword and cutting off three of her fingers, has been placed on 30 court-ordered conditions, including an ankle monitor, designed to protect the community following the end of his prison sentence.

items.title: Hundreds flee homes in Haiti amid new wave of gang violence
items.summary_auto: A new wave of gang violence in Haiti has forced hundreds of people to flee their homes, leaving them scattered on Monday along a road leading to Haiti's main airport.

items.title: Decisive Game 5 between Montreal Victoire, Minnesota Frost rescheduled to Tuesday
items.summary_auto: Game 5 between the Montreal Victoire and Minnesota Frost was postponed due to "player safety concerns related to illness."

items.title: N.B. municipalities elect new governments, including 35 new mayors
Bear euthanized after visiting Alberta summer village
items.summary_auto: A "habituated" black bear was killed by Alberta Fish and Wildlife Enforcement Services last week after reports of it frequenting a populated area. The bear was euthanized May 6 after repeatedly being seen in the west-central Alberta summer village of Burnstick Lake, about 80 kilometres southwest of Red Deer in Clearwater County.

items.title: Plan unveiled for 'sovereign AI data centre' cluster in Kamloops, Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Vancouver and Kamloops will be home to a new data centre cluster under a planned partnership announced by the federal government and Telus.

items.title: P.E.I. mothers push for amendment that would authorize bringing runaway children back home
items.summary_auto: An amendment to P.E.I. 's Child, Youth and Family Services Act would give police and child protection workers the authority to return runaway children to a safe home.

items.title: Man dies after bear encounter in northern Saskatchewan
items.summary_auto: The Ministry of Community Safety confirmed a "bear incident" Friday that resulted in the death of a 25-year-old man.

items.title: Despite profound loss, Martin Short's 'happy gene' on display in new documentary
items.summary_auto: If there's a thin line separating laughter and pain, then a new Netflix documentary out Tuesday reveals Martin Short's walked that line for most of his life.

items.title: Canada adds new sanctions on Russia over Ukrainian children's abductions
items.summary_auto: Canada is imposing new sanctions on Russia over its treatment of Ukrainian children. Russia has deported and transferred thousands of children from Ukraine to Russia or Russian-occupied territory. Many children are placed in reeducation camps or military training.

items.title: Jailed Iranian Nobel laureate's health condition 'very dangerous,' husband says
items.summary_auto: The family of Iranian Nobel Peace laureate Narges Mohammadi say she remains in critical care after she was transferred to a Tehran hospital as a result of "intensified international pressure," over her deteriorating health condition.

items.title: ICC opens arrest warrant for Philippines senator involved in Duterte's deadly war on drugs
items.summary_auto: The International Criminal Court unsealed an arrest warrant for a prominent Philippines senator linked to the deadly war on drugs overseen by ex-president Rodrigo Duterte, which allegedly involved the extrajudicial killings of suspects.
